title: Claim unemployment benefit
description: Claim a weekly payment from the National Insurance Office while you look for work.
summary: Unemployment benefit is a weekly payment from the National Insurance Office for people who have stopped working through no fault of their own. It runs for up to 26 weeks while you look for a new job.

To claim, you must have made at least 20 weeks of contributions in the 3 years before you stopped working. You must also be willing and able to take a suitable new job – the benefit is meant to support you while you look, not while you wait.

You should apply within 21 days of your last day at work. If you wait longer, you may lose some of your benefit. Bring the Termination of Services or Layoff Certificate your employer gave you, your last payslip and your bank account details.
The benefit cannot be paid at the same time as another NIS benefit. If you find work while claiming, you must tell the National Insurance Office straight away.

items: you have stopped working for reasons other than misconduct or your own choice
you have made at least 20 weeks of contributions in the 3 years before you stopped working
you are willing and able to take a suitable new job
you are not getting another NIS benefit at the same time
your National Insurance Number and National Registration Number
your last day of work and the reason you stopped working
the name and address of your last employer
your bank or credit union account details

items.name: Termination of Services or Layoff Certificate
items.note: Your former employer will give you this. See ‘Get a termination of services or layoff certificate’.

items.name: Bank or credit union account header

fees: There is no fee to claim.
how_long: A first decision usually takes 4 to 6 weeks. Payments are weekly while you are unemployed, for up to 26 weeks.

options.heading: In person
content: Take the completed Claim for Unemployment Benefit form to the Unemployment Section at the National Insurance Office.

edge_cases.heading: If you find work while claiming
content: You must tell the National Insurance Office straight away. Payments will stop on the day you start working.

related.title: Get a termination of services or layoff certificate
Set up direct deposit for NIS payments

assumptions: ‘At least 20 weeks of contributions in the 3 years before you stopped working’ is the long-standing eligibility rule. It is not stated on the form itself.
The 21-day application window is general practice. The form does not state a deadline.
‘Up to 26 weeks’ duration is the standard maximum. The form does not state the duration.
‘A first decision usually takes 4 to 6 weeks’ is an estimate.
